message.content: Domain Trust Discovery involves adversaries gathering information on domain trust relationships within Windows multi-domain/forest environments to identify lateral movement opportunities. Domain trusts enable users from one domain to access resources in another, based on mutual authentication. This information can facilitate attacks like SID-History Injection, Pass the Ticket, and Kerberoasting. Common methods for enumerating domain trusts include using the DSEnumerateDomainTrusts() Win32 API, .NET methods, LDAP, and tools like Nltest, AdFind, and PowerShell cmdlets. Notable adversaries such as APT29, Bazar, Chimera, and FIN8 have exploited these techniques. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting the use of domain enumeration tools, implementing robust logging and alerting for suspicious activities, and ensuring proper configuration and segmentation of domain trusts to minimize exposure.
